item.title:
放在办公桌下面的走路机，开始派上用场了。
我现在觉得最爽的场景就是：当下雨天吃完午饭，自己独自在家工作时，拿出走路机，拿一部美剧，午后散步四十分钟消消食，简直完美。
噪音略有点大，如果屋里有其他人...
item.description:
放在办公桌下面的走路机，开始派上用场了。
我现在觉得最爽的场景就是：当下雨天吃完午饭，自己独自在家工作时，拿出走路机，拿一部美剧，午后散步四十分钟消消食，简直完美。
噪音略有点大，如果屋里有其他人可能会有点干扰。速度上限就是 6 公里每小时，但出于安全可能就是四到五就好了。 https://t.co/x3f6XnMCcy
--- @bearliu | ❤️ 0 | 🔁 0 | 💬 0 | 👁 308
item.author: @bearliu

item.title: More connectors launching today: Adobe Creative Cloud, Ableton, Splice, Canva Affinity, SketchUp, an...
item.description:
More connectors launching today: Adobe Creative Cloud, Ableton, Splice, Canva Affinity, SketchUp, and Resolume.
We've also joined the Blender Development Fund as a patron to support open-source development of the software.
Read more: https://t.co/vg3l9MWNTg
--- @claudeai | ❤️ 274 | 🔁 21 | 💬 13 | 👁 19903
item.author: @claudeai

item.title: With the Autodesk Fusion connector, designers and engineers can create and modify 3D models through ...
item.author: @claudeai

item.title:
Claude now connects to the tools creative professionals already use.
With the new Blender connector...
item.description:
Claude now connects to the tools creative professionals already use.
With the new Blender connector, you can debug a scene, build new tools, or batch-apply changes across every object, directly from Claude. https://t.co/Kc3cBHTNpV
--- @claudeai | ❤️ 2175 | 🔁 186 | 💬 214 | 👁 92124
item.author: @claudeai

item.title:
The final insight that stood out to me:
With the right agent builder, teams can create useful AI co...
item.description:
The final insight that stood out to me:
With the right agent builder, teams can create useful AI components in minutes, not hours, using their own data.
That speed is what unlocks adoption across the org.
Watch the full video to see how modular AI works in practice.
Learn More: https://t.co/qni4eRrF96
--- @Ronald_vanLoon | ❤️ 0 | 🔁 0 | 💬 1 | 👁 90
item.author: @Ronald_vanLoon

item.title:
Why the building block approach actually scales in the real world:
→ You can compose agents instead...
item.description:
Why the building block approach actually scales in the real world:
→ You can compose agents instead of rebuilding them → You control behavior by design, not by prompt hacking → You can change one block without breaking the whole system
This is how you go from experiments to production AI.
--- @Ronald_vanLoon | ❤️ 0 | 🔁 0 | 💬 3 | 👁 101
item.author: @Ronald_vanLoon

item.title:
Most AI initiatives don’t fail because the models are weak.
They fail because teams give agents too...
item.description:
Most AI initiatives don’t fail because the models are weak.
They fail because teams give agents too much power, or not enough.
That’s the real scaling problem nobody talks about, and it’s exactly what I discussed with Arno van de Velde, Principal Solutions Architect at @elastic.
Here’s why modular AI, built like building blocks, changes the game. Let’s dig in.
#ElasticPartner #Elastic
--- @Ronald_vanLoon | ❤️ 4 | 🔁 0 | 💬 2 | 👁 146
item.author: @Ronald_vanLoon

item.title: They just use it to run a script that calculates digits of Pi.

item.title:
im writing an essay in proof using codex's in-app browser.
i type directly in the document, and cod...
item.description:
im writing an essay in proof using codex's in-app browser.
i type directly in the document, and codex loops in parallel on the left, collaborating with me in realtime.
this is obviously the future: https://t.co/4NE64zpXR9
--- @danshipper | ❤️ 78 | 🔁 5 | 💬 11 | 👁 4389
item.author: @danshipper

item.title: RT @LeRobotHF: 🤖🚀 𝗜𝗻𝘁𝗿𝗼𝗱𝘂𝗰𝗶𝗻𝗴 '𝗹𝗲𝗿𝗼𝗯𝗼𝘁-𝗿𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗼𝘂𝘁' — 𝗼𝗻𝗲 𝗖𝗟𝗜 𝘁𝗼 𝗱𝗲𝗽𝗹𝗼𝘆 𝗮𝗻𝘆 𝘁𝗿𝗮𝗶𝗻𝗲𝗱 𝗽𝗼𝗹𝗶𝗰𝘆 𝗼𝗻 𝗮𝗻𝘆 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹 𝗿...
item.description:
RT @LeRobotHF: 🤖🚀 𝗜𝗻𝘁𝗿𝗼𝗱𝘂𝗰𝗶𝗻𝗴 '𝗹𝗲𝗿𝗼𝗯𝗼𝘁-𝗿𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗼𝘂𝘁' — 𝗼𝗻𝗲 𝗖𝗟𝗜 𝘁𝗼 𝗱𝗲𝗽𝗹𝗼𝘆 𝗮𝗻𝘆 𝘁𝗿𝗮𝗶𝗻𝗲𝗱 𝗽𝗼𝗹𝗶𝗰𝘆 𝗼𝗻 𝗮𝗻𝘆 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹 𝗿𝗼𝗯𝗼𝘁.
Until today, running a trained p…
--- @Thom_Wolf | ❤️ 0 | 🔁 18 | 💬 0 | 👁 0
item.author: @Thom_Wolf

item.title: Solo AI founders tend to get stuck trying to make one product work. Here's @tibo_maker advice on wha...
item.description:
Solo AI founders tend to get stuck trying to make one product work. Here's @tibo_maker advice on what you should do instead:
"We had this crazy phase where we were shipping one new product a week until something stuck.
We were just printing the landing page, tweeting about it, and if there's no traction, we go to the next product.
Nine products failed that way and the 10th one just took off.
I would not have bet on my successful products in advance. They were a surprise to me too.
I think you need to change your mindset and see every product release as an experiment. It can totally fail, you just go to the next experiment and continue."
tl:dr Ship early and often, don't try to make it perfect.
📌 My full interview with Tibo is full of practical advice for bootstrapping AI products: https://t.co/ty2bTCUSyk
--- @petergyang | ❤️ 19 | 🔁 1 | 💬 2 | 👁 3414
item.author: @petergyang

item.title: Most people see the things around them without considering the forces that created them. In most cas...
